method: SkillOpt-adapted rollout -> judge -> validation-gate (citation tier, wave A)
held_out_queries: Roughly how many words of notes does a typical AHR article carry, against what length?
AHR target article length and how it relates to the note apparatus?

edits.summary: Quantify the otherwise-abstract 2:1 guideline with the verified concrete figure: a typical 8,000-word article (target length, excl. notes) carries ~4,500-5,500 words of notes, in both the note-density and calibration-anchor sections. CMOS exact edition left as live-check (需复核). The pre-existing 'notes grow during revision' craft note was untouched.
edits.grounded_in: official-source-map.md (verified quote: 'a typical 8,000-word article carries 4,500-5,500 words of notes')
